DEA Validation Study – Project Owner Review

This personalised link was sent because you are named as a researcher on one or more projects in the UK Statistics Authority public register. Researchers directly involved in a project may have relevant context that is not visible in the public register entry.

Participation is voluntary. After confirming that you are the intended recipient and agreeing to take part, your personalised list of projects will be displayed. You may review one, some, all or none of the listed projects, in any order.

Please do not forward this personalised link and do not enter confidential, sensitive or otherwise non-public information. Each project review normally takes approximately 3–5 minutes. Your progress is saved and you may return using the same personalised link.

This review shows short definitions beside each proposed classification. You do not need to learn the full classification framework before answering. A concise reference to all classifications is also available. You may complete all, some or none of your listed project reviews, in any order.

Research Domains
A project may have several Research Domains. Each proposed Domain should be judged independently; the Domains are not ranked.

If you later ask to withdraw this submitted project review before the deadline stated in the Participant Information Sheet, contact the study team and quote Review reference [assignment_id]. To withdraw all submitted reviews, contact the study team from the professional email address used for the invitation and state that you wish to withdraw all your submitted project reviews. You do not need to know or quote an owner identifier.

Should any additional Research Domain have been assigned?
Which additional Research Domain label or labels should have been assigned?
Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: 1, Labour Market & Employment — Work, employment, earnings, job quality, workforce dynamics, skills demand and labour-market transitions. | 2, Education & Skills — Education, learning, training, skills formation and transitions through education systems and into work. | 3, Health & Social Care — Health, illness, mental health, wellbeing, mortality as a health outcome, healthcare services and social care. | 4, Crime & Justice — Crime, victimisation, public safety, policing, courts, sentencing, prisons, probation and justice outcomes. | 5, Business & Productivity — Firms, business activity, innovation, productivity, entrepreneurship, trade, investment and business performance. | 6, Poverty, Wealth & Living Standards — Material resources, poverty, wealth, debt, benefits, deprivation, cost of living and household income. | 7, Housing & Planning — Housing, homelessness, tenure, residential conditions and mobility, neighbourhood change and planning systems. | 8, Migration & Demographics — Population structure and change, migration, fertility, ageing and mortality as a demographic outcome. | 9, Environment & Agriculture — Environment, climate, energy, agriculture, land use, pollution, decarbonisation and environmental impacts. | 10, Public Finance & Taxation — Taxation, government revenue, public spending, fiscal transfers, tax reliefs and fiscal policy. | 11, Data Infrastructure & Methodology — Data or methodology as the primary research object, rather than tools used for another question.

Field Label: Should any additional Analytical Purpose have been assigned?

The framework assigns a maximum of two Analytical Purposes to each project. Select only the most important missing purpose or purposes, taking account of any proposed purposes that you judged to fit. The resulting classification should contain no more than two purposes in total.
Which additional Analytical Purpose label or labels should have been assigned?
Choices, Calculations, OR Slider Labels: 1, Descriptive Monitoring — Measuring and describing levels, distributions, patterns or trends across places, populations or time, without primarily testing an exposure–outcome relationship. | 2, Outcome Tracking — Linking a naturally occurring exposure, condition or event to a later outcome, where the exposure is not a deliberate policy or programme. | 3, Life-Course / Trajectory Analysis — Following people, households, firms or cases over time to understand trajectories, transitions or cumulative outcomes. | 4, Service Interaction / Systems Analysis — How people, cases or organisations access, use, move through or are processed by public services. | 5, Policy Evaluation / Impact Analysis — Assessing the implementation, effects or consequences of a specific named policy, programme, regulation or intervention. | 6, Risk Prediction / Early Identification — Predicting risk or identifying at-risk people, groups or places for screening or early targeting. | 7, Methodological / Infrastructure Research — Developing, testing, validating or improving research methods, measures, linkage approaches, infrastructure or data assets.

Field Label: Please briefly explain why the selected label or labels should be included. Do not provide confidential or non-public information.
